description: Phishing templates impersonating cloud storage and file sharing services

templates.name: Dropbox File Share
suggested_subject_lines: Someone shared a file with you on Dropbox
Dropbox: New shared folder requires your access
You have a new file share from a colleague

templates.name: Google Drive Document Share
suggested_subject_lines: A document has been shared with you on Google Drive
Google Drive: Review requested — Q4 Report.xlsx
Someone shared a file: please review and comment

templates.name: Box – File Shared
suggested_subject_lines: A file has been shared with you on Box
{{.FirstName}}, you've received a file on Box
Shared with you: a document on Box
templates.notes: File-share lure for Box users. High-clicking everyday scenario.

templates.name: Google Workspace – Storage Full
suggested_subject_lines: Your Google Workspace storage is full
Action needed: you've run out of storage
You can't send or receive email until you free up space
templates.notes: Storage-full lure threatening loss of email. Effective in Google Workspace orgs.

templates.name: Adobe Acrobat – Document Shared
suggested_subject_lines: You have a document to review in Adobe Acrobat
A document has been shared with you
templates.notes: PDF-share lure via Adobe. Complements Dropbox/Google Drive/Box for a cloud-file campaign.
